questions.scenario: A change request says, `fix network timeout for orders`. Which version is ready for review?

options.text: Open inbound access to the API subnet until the timeout disappears.
Change the route table because route tables often cause timeouts.
For `snet-orders-api`, allow Application Gateway to reach port 443 from `asg-app-gateway`; verify backend health and keep the previous rule ready to restore.
Restart all backend copies and then decide whether the network still needs work.

questions.explanation: A reviewable network change names the resource, source, destination, port, verification signal, and recovery target.

questions.scenario: Why should the review record include the private DNS zone name?

options.text: Because DNS zone names prove the app code is correct.
Because a private endpoint can exist while the app still resolves the public or wrong private name.
Because Azure deletes private endpoints unless a DNS zone is present.
Because the resource group name does not matter after deployment.

questions.explanation: Private endpoint and private DNS are related but separate evidence. The team needs to know which name the app will resolve.

questions.scenario: Which details belong in a beginner network review for `devpolaris-orders-api`?

options.text: VNet, subnet, route table, NSG, and private endpoint names.
The evidence that proves the change worked after release.
Only the Azure service category because product names are enough for rollback.

questions.explanation: A useful review records the path and the proof. Product categories are too vague for rollback or incident handoff.
